sections.body_en_md:
The Biot-Savart law is the magnetic analogue of Coulomb's law: it tells us the magnetic field contribution from each tiny current element. Integrating gives the field for any current geometry.

**Exam topics:**
- Biot-Savart law formula
- Straight infinite wire
- Circular current loop (field at center and axis)
- Force between parallel wires
sections.body_he_md:
חוק ביו-סאבר הוא האנלוג המגנטי של חוק קולון: הוא נותן את תרומת השדה המגנטי מכל אלמנט זרם קטן.

**נושאי בחינה:**
- נוסחת ביו-סאבר
- חוט ישר אינסופי
- לולאת זרם מעגלית
- כוח בין חוטים מקבילים

sections.body_en_md:
**Biot-Savart law** — field contribution from current element $Id\vec{\ell}$ at position $\vec{r}$:
$$d\vec{B} = \frac{\mu_0 I}{4\pi}\frac{d\vec{\ell}\times\hat{r}}{r^2}$$

where $\hat{r}$ is the unit vector from the element to the field point, $r$ is the distance.

**Permeability of free space:** $\mu_0 = 4\pi\times10^{-7}\;\text{T·m/A}$

**Results for common geometries:**

Infinite straight wire at distance $d$:
$$B = \frac{\mu_0 I}{2\pi d}$$

Circular loop of radius $R$, field at center:
$$B = \frac{\mu_0 I}{2R}$$

Circular loop, field at distance $x$ along axis:
$$B = \frac{\mu_0 I R^2}{2(R^2+x^2)^{3/2}}$$

**Force per unit length between parallel wires** (separation $d$, currents $I_1$, $I_2$):
$$\frac{F}{L} = \frac{\mu_0 I_1 I_2}{2\pi d}$$
Attractive for same-direction currents; repulsive for opposite.
sections.body_he_md:
**ביו-סאבר:**
$$d\vec{B} = \frac{\mu_0 I}{4\pi}\frac{d\vec{\ell}\times\hat{r}}{r^2}$$

**חוט אינסופי:** $B = \mu_0 I/(2\pi d)$

**לולאה מעגלית במרכז:** $B = \mu_0 I/(2R)$

**על ציר לולאה:** $B = \mu_0 I R^2/[2(R^2+x^2)^{3/2}]$

**כוח בין חוטים:** $F/L = \mu_0 I_1 I_2/(2\pi d)$

sections.body_en_md:
### Infinite straight wire
Place wire along $z$-axis, field point at perpendicular distance $d$. Integrate $dB = \mu_0 I/(4\pi)\cdot d\ell\sin\theta/r^2$ along the wire:
$$B = \frac{\mu_0 I}{4\pi d}\int_{-\infty}^{\infty}\frac{d z}{(d^2+z^2)^{1/2}\cdot(d^2+z^2)^{1/2}}\cdot d = \frac{\mu_0 I}{2\pi d}$$
(Direction: right-hand rule — curl fingers in direction of current, thumb points in field direction)

### Circular loop at center
Every element $d\ell$ at distance $R$ contributes $dB = \mu_0 I\,d\ell/(4\pi R^2)$ perpendicular to the loop plane. No cancellation:
$$B = \frac{\mu_0 I}{4\pi R^2}\cdot 2\pi R = \frac{\mu_0 I}{2R}$$
sections.body_he_md:
### חוט ישר אינסופי
אינטגרציה על אורך החוט: $B = \mu_0 I/(2\pi d)$. כיוון: כלל יד ימין.

### לולאה מעגלית במרכז
כל אלמנט $d\ell$ תורם $dB = \mu_0 I\,d\ell/(4\pi R^2)$; אינטגרציה על היקף:
$$B = \frac{\mu_0 I}{2R}$$

sections.title_en: Worked Example 1 — Magnetic Field of Straight Wire
sections.title_he: דוגמה פתורה 1 — שדה מגנטי של חוט ישר
sections.body_en_md:
**Given:** A long straight wire carries $I = 5\;\text{A}$. Find $B$ at distance $d = 10\;\text{cm} = 0.1\;\text{m}$.

$$B = \frac{\mu_0 I}{2\pi d} = \frac{(4\pi\times10^{-7})(5)}{2\pi(0.1)} = \frac{2\times10^{-6}}{0.2} = 10^{-5}\;\text{T} = 10\;\mu\text{T}$$
A circular loop of radius $R = 5\;\text{cm}$ carries $I = 3\;\text{A}$. Find $B$ at the center.

sections.title_en: Worked Example 2 — Force Between Parallel Wires
sections.title_he: דוגמה פתורה 2 — כוח בין חוטים מקבילים
sections.body_en_md:
**Given:** Two long parallel wires, $d = 2\;\text{cm}$ apart, carry $I_1 = 10\;\text{A}$ and $I_2 = 6\;\text{A}$ in the same direction. Find the force per unit length, and state direction.

$$\frac{F}{L} = \frac{\mu_0 I_1 I_2}{2\pi d} = \frac{(4\pi\times10^{-7})(10)(6)}{2\pi(0.02)} = \frac{24\pi\times10^{-6}}{4\pi\times10^{-2}} = \frac{24\times10^{-6}}{4\times10^{-2}} = 6\times10^{-4}\;\text{N/m}$$

Since currents are in the same direction: **attractive**.
A long straight wire carries $I = 8\;\text{A}$. At what distance is $B = 16\;\mu\text{T}$?

sections.title_en: Worked Example 3 — Field on Axis of Circular Loop
sections.title_he: דוגמה פתורה 3 — שדה על ציר לולאה מעגלית
sections.body_en_md:
**Given:** A circular loop of radius $R = 0.1\;\text{m}$ carries $I = 10\;\text{A}$. Find $B$ at a point on the axis at distance $x = R = 0.1\;\text{m}$ from the center.

$$B = \frac{\mu_0 I R^2}{2(R^2 + x^2)^{3/2}}$$

With $x = R$:
$$B = \frac{\mu_0 I R^2}{2(R^2+R^2)^{3/2}} = \frac{\mu_0 I R^2}{2(2R^2)^{3/2}} = \frac{\mu_0 I R^2}{2\cdot 2\sqrt{2}R^3} = \frac{\mu_0 I}{4\sqrt{2}R}$$
$$= \frac{(4\pi\times10^{-7})(10)}{4\sqrt{2}(0.1)} = \frac{4\pi\times10^{-6}}{0.4\sqrt{2}} = \frac{\pi\times10^{-5}}{\sqrt{2}} \approx 2.22\times10^{-5}\;\text{T}$$

| Geometry | Formula | Direction |
|---|---|---|
| Infinite straight wire | $B = \mu_0 I/(2\pi d)$ | Right-hand rule (circle around wire) |
| Finite wire segment | Integrate Biot-Savart or use angle formula | Right-hand rule |
| Circular loop (center) | $B = \mu_0 I/(2R)$ | Right-hand rule (curl fingers in current direction) |
| Circular loop (axis) | $B = \mu_0 IR^2/[2(R^2+x^2)^{3/2}]$ | Along axis |
| Parallel wires (force/length) | $F/L = \mu_0 I_1 I_2/(2\pi d)$ | Attract: same direction; repel: opposite |
sections.body_he_md:
| גיאומטריה | נוסחה |
|---|---|
| חוט אינסופי | $B = \mu_0 I/(2\pi d)$ |
| לולאה — מרכז | $B = \mu_0 I/(2R)$ |
| לולאה — ציר | $B = \mu_0 IR^2/[2(R^2+x^2)^{3/2}]$ |
| כוח בין חוטים | $F/L = \mu_0 I_1 I_2/(2\pi d)$ |

exercises.body_en: A long wire carries $I = 20\;\text{A}$. Find $B$ at $d = 5\;\text{cm}$.
A circular loop ($R = 8\;\text{cm}$, $I = 5\;\text{A}$). Find $B$ at center.
Two parallel wires carry $I_1 = 4\;\text{A}$ and $I_2 = 6\;\text{A}$ in opposite directions, $d = 3\;\text{cm}$ apart. Find force per unit length and state if attractive or repulsive.
exercises.body_he: שני חוטים $I_1 = 4$, $I_2 = 6\;\text{A}$ כיוונים הפוכים, $d = 3\;\text{cm}$. מצא כוח לאורך יחידה.
exercises.solution_en: $F/L = \mu_0 I_1 I_2/(2\pi d) = (4\pi\times10^{-7})(24)/(2\pi\times0.03) = (4\times24\times10^{-7})/(2\times0.03) = 96\times10^{-7}/0.06 = 1.6\times10^{-4}\;\text{N/m}$. Opposite currents → repulsive.

exercises.body_en: What is $\mu_0$? State its value and units.

A wire of length $L = 0.4\;\text{m}$ carries $I = 15\;\text{A}$. Find the field at a perpendicular distance $d = 2\;\text{cm}$ from the midpoint of the wire (use the result for a finite wire: $B = \mu_0 I\sin\theta_{\max}/(2\pi d)$ where $\theta_{\max} = \arctan(L/2d)$).
exercises.solution_en: $\theta_{\max} = \arctan((0.2)/0.02) = \arctan(10) \approx 84.3°$. $\sin\theta_{\max} \approx 0.995$. $B = \mu_0 I\sin\theta_{\max}/(\pi d) = (4\pi\times10^{-7})(15)(0.995)/(\pi\times0.02) = (4\times15\times0.995\times10^{-7})/0.02 = 59.7\times10^{-7}/0.02 \approx 2.98\times10^{-4}\;\text{T}$. (Compare infinite wire: $\mu_0 I/(2\pi d) = 3\times10^{-4}\;\text{T}$; very close.

exercises.body_en: Two circular loops (same radius $R = 5\;\text{cm}$, same current $I = 2\;\text{A}$) are coaxial and separated by $d = 5\;\text{cm}$. Find the field at the midpoint between them (on the axis).
exercises.body_he: שתי לולאות ($R = 5\;\text{cm}$, $I = 2\;\text{A}$) קואציאליות, $d = 5\;\text{cm}$ ביניהן. מצא שדה בנקודת האמצע.

exercises.body_en: Current $I = 12\;\text{A}$ flows in a square loop (side $a = 10\;\text{cm}$). Find the field at the center (use result for finite wire: $B = \mu_0 I/(\sqrt{2}\pi a)$ per side).
exercises.solution_en: For a finite wire of length $a$ at perpendicular distance $a/2$ from center: $B_{\text{side}} = \mu_0 I/(2\pi(a/2))\times\sin(45°)\times2 = \mu_0 I/(\pi a)\times(\sqrt{2}/2) = \mu_0 I\sqrt{2}/(2\pi a)$... more precisely, per side: $B = \mu_0 I\sqrt{2}/(2\pi a)$. Four sides: $B_{\text{total}} = 4\times\frac{\mu_0 I\sqrt{2}}{2\pi a} = \frac{2\sqrt{2}\mu_0 I}{\pi a} = \frac{2\sqrt{2}(4\pi\times10^{-7})(12)}{\pi(0.1)} = \frac{2\sqrt{2}(4.8\times10^{-6})}{1} = 2\sqrt{2}\times4.8\times10^{-6} \approx 13.6\times10^{-6}\;\text{T} \approx 13.6\;\mu\text{T}$.

exercises.body_en: The Earth's magnetic field at the surface is $B \approx 50\;\mu\text{T}$. What current in a long straight wire at $d = 10\;\text{m}$ would produce this field?
exercises.body_he: השדה המגנטי של כדור הארץ $B \approx 50\;\mu\text{T}$. איזה זרם בחוט ישר במרחק $d = 10\;\text{m}$ יפיק שדה זה?

exercises.body_en: A solenoid has $n = 1000\;\text{turns/m}$ and carries $I = 3\;\text{A}$. Find field inside (use $B = \mu_0 n I$).
Derive the magnetic field at the center of a circular loop using the Biot-Savart law.
exercises.body_he: גזור שדה מגנטי במרכז לולאה מעגלית על-ידי חוק ביו-סאבר.
exercises.solution_en: Each element $d\ell = R\,d\phi$ at the rim is at distance $R$ from the center. The cross product $d\vec{\ell}\times\hat{r}$ is perpendicular to both — for a circle, it always points along the axis (say $\hat{z}$) with magnitude $d\ell$. So $dB = \mu_0 I\,d\ell/(4\pi R^2)$. Integrating around the full circle: $B = \mu_0 I/(4\pi R^2)\times 2\pi R = \mu_0 I/(2R)$ ∎
exercises.solution_he: $d\vec{\ell}\times\hat{r}$: גודל $d\ell$, כיוון ציר. אינטגרציה: $B = \mu_0 I\cdot 2\pi R/(4\pi R^2) = \mu_0 I/(2R)$ ∎

exercises.body_en: Two long parallel wires carry currents $I$ in opposite directions, separated by distance $2a$. Find the field at a point equidistant from both wires, at perpendicular distance $d$ from the midpoint (where $d \gg a$).
exercises.body_he: שני חוטים מקבילים ישאים זרם $I$ בכיוונים הפוכים, ביניהם $2a$. מצא שדה בנקודה במרחק $d$ מנקודת האמצע ($d \gg a$).
exercises.solution_en: Each wire creates $B = \mu_0 I/(2\pi r)$ at distance $r \approx d$. For opposite currents, the fields add at the midpoint (both point in the same direction at points along the perpendicular bisector, actually they are in opposite horizontal directions but both bend same way... more carefully: wire 1 at $x=+a$, wire 2 at $x=-a$, I_1 in $+z$, I_2 in $-z$. At point $(0,d,0)$: wire 1 field = $\mu_0 I/(2\pi r_1)$ in direction $\hat{z}\times(-\hat{r}_1)$; for $d \gg a$: $r_1 \approx r_2 \approx d$. Net field (using $a \ll d$) $\approx \mu_0 I a/(\pi d^2)$ pointing perpendicular to line joining wires.

exercises.body_en: Show that on the axis of a circular loop, at $x \gg R$, the field behaves as a magnetic dipole: $B \approx \mu_0 m/(2\pi x^3)$ where $m = I\pi R^2$.
exercises.body_he: הראה שעל ציר לולאה, עבור $x \gg R$, השדה מתנהג כדיפול מגנטי: $B \approx \mu_0 m/(2\pi x^3)$.
exercises.solution_en: $B = \mu_0 I R^2/[2(R^2+x^2)^{3/2}]$. For $x \gg R$: $(R^2+x^2)^{3/2} \approx x^3$. $B \approx \mu_0 I R^2/(2x^3) = \mu_0(I\pi R^2)/(2\pi x^3) = \mu_0 m/(2\pi x^3)$ where $m = I\pi R^2$ is the magnetic dipole moment ∎
exercises.solution_he: $x \gg R$: $(R^2+x^2)^{3/2} \approx x^3$; $B \approx \mu_0 IR^2/(2x^3) = \mu_0 m/(2\pi x^3)$ כאשר $m = I\pi R^2$ ∎

exercises.body_en: A rectangular current loop (sides $a = 4\;\text{cm}$, $b = 6\;\text{cm}$, $I = 5\;\text{A}$) is in a uniform field $B = 0.3\;\text{T}$. Find the maximum torque on the loop.
exercises.body_he: לולאה מלבנית ($a = 4\;\text{cm}$, $b = 6\;\text{cm}$, $I = 5\;\text{A}$) בשדה $B = 0.3\;\text{T}$. מצא מומנט סיבוב מקסימלי.
exercises.solution_en: Magnetic moment: $m = Iab = 5(0.04)(0.06) = 0.012\;\text{A·m}^2$. Max torque (when $m \perp B$): $\tau = mB = 0.012\times0.3 = 3.6\times10^{-3}\;\text{N·m}$.

sections.body_en_md:
1. **$B = \mu_0 I/(2\pi d)$ is for an INFINITE wire.** For a finite wire, the formula is different.

2. **Cross product in Biot-Savart.** $d\vec{\ell}\times\hat{r}$: magnitude = $d\ell\sin\alpha$ (angle between $d\vec{\ell}$ and $\hat{r}$); direction by right-hand rule.

3. **Parallel wires with same current attract.** This is often confused — same-direction currents attract; opposite-direction repel.

4. **Superposition.** For multiple wires, add fields as vectors, not scalars.
sections.body_he_md:
1. **$B = \mu_0 I/(2\pi d)$ — לחוט אינסופי בלבד.**

2. **מכפלה וקטורית:** $|d\vec{\ell}\times\hat{r}| = d\ell\sin\alpha$.

3. **חוטים מקבילים עם זרם אחד — **משיכה**.**

4. **סופרפוזיציה:** חבר שדות כוקטורים.

sections.body_en_md:
- **Biot-Savart:** $d\vec{B} = \frac{\mu_0 I}{4\pi}\frac{d\vec{\ell}\times\hat{r}}{r^2}$
- **Infinite wire:** $B = \mu_0 I/(2\pi d)$
- **Loop center:** $B = \mu_0 I/(2R)$
- **Loop axis:** $B = \mu_0 IR^2/[2(R^2+x^2)^{3/2}]$
- **Solenoid inside:** $B = \mu_0 nI$
- **Force/length, parallel wires:** $F/L = \mu_0 I_1 I_2/(2\pi d)$
- $\mu_0 = 4\pi\times10^{-7}\;\text{T·m/A}$
sections.body_he_md:
- חוט אינסופי: $B = \mu_0 I/(2\pi d)$
- לולאה מרכז: $B = \mu_0 I/(2R)$
- סולנואיד: $B = \mu_0 nI$
- כוח בין חוטים: $F/L = \mu_0 I_1 I_2/(2\pi d)$

sections.body_en_md:
- **Biot-Savart:** $d\vec{B} = \frac{\mu_0 I}{4\pi}\frac{d\vec{\ell}\times\hat{r}}{r^2}$ — integrate for full field
- **Infinite wire:** $B = \mu_0 I/(2\pi d)$; circles around wire (right-hand rule)
- **Loop center:** $B = \mu_0 I/(2R)$
- **Parallel wires:** attract (same direction), repel (opposite); $F/L = \mu_0 I_1 I_2/(2\pi d)$
sections.body_he_md:
- ביו-סאבר: $d\vec{B} = \frac{\mu_0 I}{4\pi}\frac{d\vec{\ell}\times\hat{r}}{r^2}$
- חוט אינסופי: $B = \mu_0 I/(2\pi d)$
- לולאה: $B = \mu_0 I/(2R)$
- חוטים מקבילים: $F/L = \mu_0 I_1 I_2/(2\pi d)$

questions.stem_en: A long straight wire carries current $I$. The magnetic field at distance $d$ is proportional to:
questions.stem_he: חוט ישר ארוך נושא זרם $I$. השדה המגנטי במרחק $d$ פרופורציוני ל:

questions.stem_en: Find $B$ at the center of a circular loop ($R = 0.1\;\text{m}$, $I = 10\;\text{A}$) in $\mu\text{T}$.

key_insights: Force between parallel wires: $F/L = \mu_0 I_1 I_2/(2\pi d)$; same direction → attractive.

common_misconceptions.wrong: Magnetic field of a wire decreases as $1/r^2$ like electric field of a point charge
common_misconceptions.correction: For an infinite line current, $B \propto 1/r$ (not $1/r^2$). It's a line source, so the geometry changes the falloff.
common_misconceptions.detect_phrase_he: שדה חוט אחד על r בריבוע

agent_hints.tutor_pacing_hint: Start with the formula and right-hand rule for infinite wire — most common calculation. Then circular loop. Emphasize the cross product direction. Force between wires is a standard exam problem.
